name: Jerzy Dudek
role_2004_05: First-choice goalkeeper for Liverpool's knockout run and final, starting nine matches including the showpiece in Istanbul. He became a club legend for his point-blank double save against Shevchenko in extra time and his match-winning heroics in the penalty shootout.
qualitative_descriptor: A reflex-oriented goalkeeper capable of both spectacular heroics and erratic handling within the same match. He excelled in one-on-one shot-stopping and high-pressure situations, as evidenced by his legendary 'wobbly legs' routine during the 2005 final shootout. While an athletic and rangy presence, his consistency and command of the area could occasionally be streaky.

name: Steve Finnan
role_2004_05: Finnan was the trusted defensive right-back throughout the Champions League campaign, starting every knockout match including the final. He was a disciplined pillar of the side that survived Juventus and Chelsea, though he was sacrificed at half-time in Istanbul during the tactical shift to a back three.
qualitative_descriptor: A dependable and positionally disciplined full-back who provided consistent stability for Rafael Benítez. While not a flashy or explosive runner, he possessed high stamina and a reliable crossing ability that complemented his primary focus on defensive organisation. His calm, low-risk approach made him a quintessential role player for a top-flight side in high-pressure European ties.

name: Sami Hyypiä
role_2004_05: As a senior defensive leader and ever-present figure, Hyypiä anchored the back line throughout the knockout stages and final. He served as the club's captain whenever Steven Gerrard was absent and scored the crucial opening goal in the quarter-final first leg against Juventus.
qualitative_descriptor: A dominant aerial centre-back with elite positioning and anticipation, Hyypiä was a formidable presence in both penalty boxes. While his turning speed and acceleration were limited, his ability to read danger early allowed him to lead the defence through high-pressure scenarios. He was exceptionally strong in the air and served as a tactical pillar in Rafael Benítez’s organised defensive system.

name: Milan Baroš
role_2004_05: Baroš served as Liverpool's primary centre-forward and first-choice runner throughout the campaign, starting the final in Istanbul. He was a key tactical outlet, notably providing the assist for Steven Gerrard to win the equalising penalty in the final.
qualitative_descriptor: A direct and industrious striker known for his ability to stretch defences by running into the channels. He excels when dribbling at speed and taking on defenders, though his finishing can be erratic at times. His high work rate and willingness to challenge defenders made him a vital component of Benítez's counter-attacking system.

name: John Arne Riise
role_2004_05: Riise was Liverpool's primary left-back during the campaign, appearing in every match of the knockout run and starting the final. He provided a critical attacking outlet and provided the assist for Steven Gerrard's goal that sparked the second-half comeback in Istanbul.
qualitative_descriptor: A high-energy left-sided player with a powerful engine and a devastating left foot, Riise was a major threat from long-range shooting and set-pieces. While his direct running and crossing made him a dangerous offensive presence, his first touch could occasionally be loose. He was versatile enough to operate as a full-back, wing-back, or left midfielder depending on Benítez's tactical requirements.

name: Harry Kewell
role_2004_05: Kewell had an injury-hit season but remained a trusted creative option for Benítez when available, culminating in a surprise start in the Champions League final.
qualitative_descriptor: A creative support forward and left-sided dribbler known for his excellent close control and danger around the penalty area. While technically gifted and capable of unlocking defences, his impact during this campaign was significantly limited by recurring fitness issues. His season ended prematurely after just 23 minutes of the final in Istanbul due to an adductor tear.

name: Steven Gerrard
role_2004_05: Club captain and emotional centre of the side who scored in the final and the vital group-stage decider against Olympiacos. He served as the team's driving force, famously shifting to a more advanced attacking role during the second half in Istanbul to spark the comeback.
qualitative_descriptor: A complete box-to-box midfielder defined by his elite leadership, high-energy drive in transition, and excellent crossing range. He possessed a lethal long-range shot and the tactical versatility to influence games from central, right, or attacking midfield positions. His ability to deliver decisive contributions in high-pressure moments made him the campaign's most iconic figure.

name: Djibril Cissé
role_2004_05: As a marquee summer signing, Cissé scored on his European debut before suffering a horrific double leg fracture in October. He made a remarkable recovery to return for the final three matches of the campaign, ultimately converting a penalty in the shootout victory in Istanbul.
qualitative_descriptor: An explosive and direct centre-forward defined by outstanding natural pace and a very powerful shot. While his link play could be volatile and technically inconsistent, he remained a high-leverage attacking threat capable of stretching defences in transition. His physical resilience was demonstrated by his rapid return from a tibia and fibula fracture to contribute to the club's fifth European Cup.

name: Luis García
role_2004_05: García was Liverpool's primary match-winner in the knockout stages, scoring five crucial goals including the decider in the semi-final and a double against Bayer Leverkusen. He started the final in Istanbul as a creative second striker before moving to the right flank following the half-time tactical reshuffle.
qualitative_descriptor: A technically gifted second striker or inside forward, García was renowned for his opportunistic finishing and intelligent movement between the lines. He possessed excellent close control and a knack for scoring spectacular, high-leverage goals in European competition. While relatively light physically, his ability to ghost into goalscoring positions made him a constant threat in Benítez's counter-attacking system.

name: Vladimír Šmicer
role_2004_05: A versatile squad player who provided vital experience and technical quality from the bench, most famously replacing the injured Harry Kewell early in the final. He scored Liverpool's second goal in Istanbul and converted his penalty in the shootout during his final appearance for the club.
qualitative_descriptor: An intelligent attacking midfielder known for his technical neatness, composed finishing, and ability to make well-timed late runs into the box. While not a regular starter during the 2004/05 campaign, he remained a reliable big-game performer with a proven knack for contributing in high-leverage moments. His primary strengths lay in his tactical awareness and link-up play, though he lacked elite explosive pace at this stage of his career.

name: Anthony Le Tallec
role_2004_05: A technical squad player who provided depth in the forward line, famously starting the quarter-final first leg against Juventus at Anfield.
qualitative_descriptor: A technical support striker noted for his tidy link play and refined first touch. While possessing good vision and ball control, he lacked the explosive pace and physical dominance of elite contemporary forwards, making him better suited to a creative second-striker role than leading the line alone.

name: Xabi Alonso
role_2004_05: Alonso served as Liverpool's deep-lying playmaker, returning from a long ankle injury lay-off to anchor the midfield during the successful quarter-final, semi-final, and final. He scored the vital equaliser in the Istanbul final by converted the rebound of his own saved penalty.
qualitative_descriptor: An outstanding deep playmaker known for his exceptional passing range and ability to control the tempo of a match. He offers significant set-piece quality and dictates rhythm from deep positions, though he lacks elite acceleration. While highly intelligent in his positioning, he relies more on his vision and distribution than physical burst to dominate the midfield.

name: Salif Diao
role_2004_05: Diao was a fringe squad player for Liverpool during their Champions League campaign, making only a brief cameo appearance as a late substitute against Olympiacos in the group stage.
qualitative_descriptor: A physical holding midfielder who prioritises defensive duties, such as tackling and covering ground. He is a simple passer of the ball with limited creativity, primarily serving as a depth option to provide energy and screening in front of the back four.

name: Dietmar Hamann
role_2004_05: A senior holding midfielder who provided tactical intelligence and screening for the Liverpool midfield. He was the defining substitute of the season, with his half-time introduction in the Champions League final transforming the team's structure and stifling Milan's creative threat.
qualitative_descriptor: A quintessential holding midfielder defined by his tactical intelligence and calmness under pressure. While lacking significant mobility or pace, his ability to read the game and provide a screen for the back four was elite. He was a role-player of the highest reliability, capable of maintaining possession and restoring defensive balance in high-leverage knockout football.

name: Josémi
role_2004_05: An early-season regular at right-back after joining from Málaga, he eventually lost his starting place to Steve Finnan during the knockout stages. He remained a frequent substitute, notably appearing in the final group-stage win over Olympiacos and the first leg against Deportivo.
qualitative_descriptor: A safer than spectacular full-back who prioritises defensive stability over attacking flair. He possesses moderate pace and defensive-first instincts, making him a reliable but limited option on the right flank. While physically useful in duels, his contribution to the final third is restricted by a lack of elite offensive upside.

name: Antonio Núñez
role_2004_05: A right-sided depth option who served as a squad player during Liverpool's Champions League campaign. He appeared as a substitute in key knockout matches against Bayer Leverkusen, Juventus, and Chelsea, and started the decisive final group game against Olympiacos.
qualitative_descriptor: A hardworking right-sided midfielder noted for his crossing ability and defensive effort. While he provided reliable tactical discipline and work rate as a role player, he lacked the top-level explosiveness or creative end product to secure a regular starting position. He was primarily utilised by Rafael Benítez to provide fresh legs and stability on the right flank during the latter stages of matches.

name: Scott Carson
role_2004_05: Carson served as a young reserve goalkeeper following his move from Leeds United in early 2005, making a vital start in the Champions League quarter-final first leg against Juventus.
qualitative_descriptor: A raw but composed young goalkeeper with a strong frame and promising handling. He displayed good shot-stopping abilities and composure in his limited high-stakes appearances, though he remained third-choice behind Jerzy Dudek and Chris Kirkland during the European run.

name: Djimi Traoré
role_2004_05: Traor� played 10 Champions League matches and was the starting left-back in the final in Istanbul. He was a trusted defensive presence throughout the knockout stages, starting in crucial ties against Deportivo, Leverkusen, Juventus, and Chelsea.
qualitative_descriptor: A defender valued for his recovery pace and significant tackling reach, Traor� was physically useful in individual duels. While a capable athlete, his concentration could occasionally wobble under sustained pressure. He was a functional role player who provided Ben�tez with the necessary defensive attributes to execute a containment-heavy strategy.

name: Chris Kirkland
role_2004_05: Kirkland began the season as a frequent starter in the Champions League, making four appearances during the group stage. He notably kept goal during critical home and away fixtures against Deportivo La Coru!a and Monaco before injury issues saw him lose his place to Jerzy Dudek for the knockout phase.
qualitative_descriptor: Kirkland is an agile shot-stopper with excellent reactions and a conservative approach to positioning. He is generally less commanding of his area than other senior goalkeepers, preferring to remain on his line rather than aggressively claim crosses. While reliable in terms of technical handling and shot-stopping, his impact was often limited by the physical durability issues that plagued his campaign.

name: Jamie Carragher
role_2004_05: An ever-present pillar of the defence who transitioned from a utility role to a specialist centre-back, playing every minute of the Champions League campaign.
qualitative_descriptor: An aggressive and resilient stopper known for his leadership and exceptional recovery commitment, notably playing through severe cramp during the Istanbul final. While his progressive passing is ordinary, he is an elite reader of danger and a senior leader in the defensive spine.

name: Florent Sinama-Pongolle
role_2004_05: A youthful forward used primarily as an impact substitute, he proved vital by scoring the first goal in the 3-1 comeback victory over Olympiacos that secured qualification from the group stage.
qualitative_descriptor: A direct and fast attacker who thrives when running at defenders with pace. While his final ball and decision-making can be raw, he provides significant chaos value when introduced off the bench against tiring defences.

name: Igor Bišćan
role_2004_05: Bišćan served as a tactical utility option and reliable midfield depth, appearing in several high-stakes knockout ties including both legs of the quarter-final against Juventus and semi-final against Chelsea. He provided essential defensive stability and height in midfield during Liverpool's path to the final.
qualitative_descriptor: An athletic and positionally obedient utility player capable of operating as a central midfielder, defensive specialist, or auxiliary centre-back. While he lacks elite creative invention, he is noted for being a tidy distributor and a disciplined worker who follows tactical instructions. His physical profile and aerial presence made him a trusted selection for Rafael Benítez when seeking to contain high-calibre European opposition.

name: Stephen Warnock
role_2004_05: A versatile academy product who served as a useful squad player, making several appearances at left-back and left midfield during the campaign. He was trusted with starts in difficult away fixtures against Olympiacos and Bayer Leverkusen.
qualitative_descriptor: An honest and mobile left-sided defender known for his steady crossing and high work rate. He provided functional depth to the squad, though he lacked the elite attributes of the first-choice options. His play was characterised by dependable effort and a professional approach to his defensive duties.

name: John Welsh
role_2004_05: A young academy graduate who served as a depth option in midfield, making a late substitute appearance in the 3-1 round of 16 second-leg win away at Bayer Leverkusen.
qualitative_descriptor: A combative utility midfielder known for his energetic playing style and simple, effective distribution. While useful as a squad depth option, he lacked the technical refinement of a primary conductor at the elite European level. He provided a physically reliable presence in the middle of the pitch when called upon to see out games.

name: Neil Mellor
role_2004_05: A young academy striker who provided crucial relief during an injury crisis, famously scoring and assisting in the group-stage decider against Olympiacos.
qualitative_descriptor: An instinctive penalty-box striker known for his finishing and ability to link play. While he lacked elite pace over long distances, his movement and opportunistic scoring made him an effective rotation option during the group stages.

name: Darren Potter
role_2004_05: A young academy graduate who served as a depth option in midfield, making a late substitute appearance in the Round of 16 first leg against Bayer Leverkusen.
qualitative_descriptor: A technical left-footed midfielder who demonstrated composure and calm in possession during his limited first-team opportunities. At this stage of his development, he lacked the top-level physical power and explosive burst required to challenge for a regular starting role in Benítez’s established midfield rotation.

name: Dida
role_2004_05: The first-choice goalkeeper throughout Milan's run to the final, appearing in every major tie of the campaign. He kept clean sheets in both legs against Manchester United and Internazionale, and notably saved a penalty from Xabi Alonso in the final.
qualitative_descriptor: A tall, rangy goalkeeper known for elite reflexes and an exceptional reach that made him a formidable shot-stopper. He provided a calm, physically imposing presence in the box, though his rebound control could occasionally vary. During this period, he was widely regarded as one of the most reliable and athletic keepers in world football.

name: Cafu
role_2004_05: A veteran attacking right-back and key starter who provided relentless energy on the flank as Milan reached the final. He was a constant overlapping outlet in Ancelotti's diamond system, starting the Istanbul final as the primary source of right-sided width.
qualitative_descriptor: A relentless attacking full-back defined by high stamina and constant overlapping runs. He possesses excellent crossing ability and maintains world-class recovery habits despite his age. While primarily an offensive threat, he remains a disciplined and experienced defender in individual duels.

name: Paolo Maldini
role_2004_05: As Milan's captain and defensive anchor, he led the side to the final in Istanbul and famously scored the opening goal after only 51 seconds. He remained a world-class presence at centre-back throughout the campaign, forming an elite partnership with Alessandro Nesta.
qualitative_descriptor: An elite reader of danger and elegant duelist, Maldini retained huge authority and world-class positioning despite his ageing legs. While his top-end speed had naturally declined by 36, his anticipation and ability to play at both centre-back and left-back made him an indispensable leader. He combined technical composure on the ball with an unrivalled ability to neutralise opposition threats through timing and intelligence.

name: Kakha Kaladze
role_2004_05: A versatile defensive rotation player, Kaladze served as a reliable option across both left-back and centre-back during Milan's run to the Istanbul final. He was part of a star-studded defensive unit, providing essential depth and tactical flexibility behind the primary starters.
qualitative_descriptor: Kaladze was a balanced and physically strong defender who combined defensive stability with technical competence on the ball. While not as explosive an attacking outlet as Cafu or Serginho, his ability to read the game and slot seamlessly into multiple positions made him a trusted component of Ancelotti's squad. He was a reliable, low-risk performer who prioritised defensive positioning over spectacular individual play.

name: Alessandro Costacurta
role_2004_05: A veteran reserve centre-back and vice-captain who provided experienced defensive cover throughout the Champions League campaign.
qualitative_descriptor: An elite veteran organiser who relies on his world-class reading of the game to intercept danger before it develops. While he possessed very limited pace at this late stage of his career, he remained a highly reliable squad option due to his superb positioning and tactical intelligence.

name: Andriy Shevchenko
role_2004_05: The reigning 2004 Ballon d’Or winner and Milan's spearhead, he was an ever-present threat throughout the run to the final. Though he assisted Crespo's first goal in Istanbul, the season ended in heartbreak as his 117th-minute shot was saved by Dudek and he missed the deciding penalty in the shootout.
qualitative_descriptor: A complete, elite all-round striker who combined blistering pace with clinical movement and timing. He was equally dangerous as a transition weapon in open space or as a predatory finisher within the penalty box. While possessing no significant technical weaknesses, his season is defined by the narrowest of margins in the Istanbul final.

name: Gennaro Gattuso
role_2004_05: Gattuso was Milan's primary defensive engine, starting the 2005 final in the midfield diamond and providing the necessary physical cover for Andrea Pirlo. He was a near-constant presence in a campaign that saw Milan eliminate Manchester United and Inter on their way to Istanbul.
qualitative_descriptor: A relentless ball-winner and pressing specialist known for his high intensity and emotional leadership on the pitch. While his distribution is limited to simple and effective circulation, his ability to destroy opposition rhythm through constant pressure and successful duels makes him an elite defensive foil. He serves as the team's catalyst for work rate, though his aggressive style can lead to significant foul counts.

name: Filippo Inzaghi
role_2004_05: Inzaghi had an injury-disrupted campaign, making only two Champions League appearances. He returned to fitness late in the season and made the bench for the final, but was not included in the matchday squad for the showpiece in Istanbul.
qualitative_descriptor: A quintessential poacher whose game is defined by his movement on the shoulder of the last defender. He is an offside-line specialist with elite anticipation and instinctive finishing inside the penalty area. While he offers minimal contribution to general play or build-up, his ability to convert half-chances makes him a persistent goal threat.

name: Rui Costa
role_2004_05: Rui Costa served as a rotational playmaker and technical alternative to Kaká, often used as a technical change off the bench including a late appearance in the Istanbul final.
qualitative_descriptor: A classic number 10 defined by exceptional vision, disguise, and creative passing. While he operated at a softer tempo than the more explosive Kaká, he remained an ideal option for unlocking deep defensive blocks. His game relied on technical elegance and football intelligence rather than physical dominance or burst.

name: Hernán Crespo
role_2004_05: A loan signing from Chelsea, Crespo was a vital attacking component for Milan, most notably scoring two elegant goals in the 2005 Champions League final.
qualitative_descriptor: A specialist penalty-box finisher, Crespo is defined by his elite poacher's movement and exceptionally quick first touch. While less of a self-creating threat than strike partner Shevchenko, his predatory instincts and clinical finishing make him a constant danger in the area. He possesses the technical quality for delicate finishes, though he relies heavily on service and vertical through balls to be effective.

name: Alessandro Nesta
role_2004_05: An ever-present elite stopper in Milan's major knockout ties, Nesta formed a formidable central defensive partnership with Paolo Maldini and Jaap Stam. He was a cornerstone of a defence that reached the final in Istanbul, starting as the right-sided centre-back.
qualitative_descriptor: An anticipatory centre-back defined by superb timing in his tackles and elite cover defending. He possesses the necessary pace to cover ground effectively and is highly regarded for his ability to read danger before it develops. His game is built on elegance and composure rather than raw physicality.

name: Jon Dahl Tomasson
role_2004_05: A reliable rotational forward who provided depth for Milan's star-studded attack, famously appearing as a late substitute for Hernán Crespo in the final and converting his penalty in the shootout.
qualitative_descriptor: Tomasson was a highly intelligent support forward known for his clever movement and link-up play. He excelled at finding space in congested areas and was respected for his work rate and unselfishness. While not as explosive as Shevchenko or as clinical as Inzaghi, his tactical discipline made him an ideal squad player for a top-tier side.

name: Christian Abbiati
role_2004_05: Abbiati served as the primary backup to Dida, making only a single appearance during AC Milan's run to the 2005 Champions League final.
qualitative_descriptor: An athletic and proactive goalkeeper, Abbiati was a reliable alternative who was known for his shot-stopping ability and willingness to come off his line. While a capable deputy, he was generally considered slightly less controlled in his positioning than the first-choice Dida. He provided experienced cover within a squad that reached the final in Istanbul.

name: Fabricio Coloccini
role_2004_05: A young depth defender who made a single group-stage appearance for Milan before departing mid-season.
qualitative_descriptor: A front-foot defender characterized by an assertive and proactive approach to winning the ball. He is decent in possession and possesses significant developmental upside, though he can occasionally be rash in his decision-making. At this stage of his career, he offered raw physical tools rather than the polished control seen in Milan's veteran starters.

name: Clarence Seedorf
role_2004_05: Seedorf was a foundational member of Milan's starting XI, serving as a physically powerful controller and creator in the midfield. He started the 2005 final in Istanbul and was a key presence throughout the knockout stages against Manchester United, Inter, and PSV.
qualitative_descriptor: Seedorf is a physically strong controller capable of dribbling out of intense pressure and maintaining excellent ball security. He combines tactical intelligence with a powerful long-range shot and the technical ability to operate as both a central and attacking midfielder. His presence allows his team to dictate rhythm and transition smoothly into attack.

name: Andrea Pirlo
role_2004_05: As Milan's quintessential 'regista', Pirlo was the technical heartbeat of the side, dictating tempo and providing the assist for Maldini's opening goal in the 2005 final.
qualitative_descriptor: A visionary deep-lying playmaker, Pirlo possessed exceptional long and medium passing range that allowed him to control the rhythm of elite matches. While not a natural ball-winner, his elite dead-ball delivery and composure under pressure made him the primary architect of Milan's attacking transitions.

name: Kaká
role_2004_05: A central creative force for AC Milan, Kaká started the 2005 final and provided the assist for Milan's third goal before converting his penalty in the shootout.
qualitative_descriptor: A long-striding attacking midfielder renowned as a transition killer with devastating acceleration while in possession. He possesses an incisive final pass and the ability to dominate the space between the lines, though his influence can be stifled by disciplined tactical marking.

name: Massimo Ambrosini
role_2004_05: Ambrosini was a vital physical and aerial midfield rotation option for Carlo Ancelotti, often used to add steel and height in major European matches. He notably scored the decisive late away goal against PSV Eindhoven in the semi-final second leg to secure Milan's place in the final.
qualitative_descriptor: A powerful box-to-box or defensive midfielder known for his immense aerial prowess and physical dominance in central duels. He was a specialist in making late runs into the penalty area and provided a rugged, high-volume tackling presence that contrasted with Milan's more technical playmakers. While lacking the elite passing range of a regista, his tactical discipline and engine made him a high-value squad member for big-game scenarios.

name: Vikash Dhorasoo
role_2004_05: A summer signing from Lyon who served as a technical midfield option, often utilised as a substitute to provide control and mobility during Milan's run to the final.
qualitative_descriptor: Dhorasoo is a technical passer and mobile controller who is particularly effective when operating between the lines. He is noted for being light-footed and comfortable in possession, though he is less physically dominant than other options in the Milan midfield.

name: Giuseppe Pancaro
role_2004_05: A veteran depth defender who provided cover for the primary full-backs. He was a fringe squad member in Europe, making only one Champions League appearance during the campaign.
qualitative_descriptor: Pancaro was an experienced and sturdy defensive full-back capable of operating on either flank. He was a low-risk, reliable professional valued for his positioning and defensive-first instincts rather than his attacking output.

name: Serginho
role_2004_05: A reliable rotational wide player, Serginho was a high-value substitute who made a late appearance in the Champions League final.
qualitative_descriptor: An explosive left-sided outlet, Serginho was known for his direct acceleration and ability to stretch opposition defences. He offered dangerous crossing and chaos value off the bench, though his performance could occasionally be inconsistent.

name: Jaap Stam
role_2004_05: A major summer signing from Lazio, Stam was a primary fixture in Milan's star-studded defence during their run to the 2005 final. He started the showpiece in Istanbul as part of a veteran back four alongside Maldini, Nesta, and Cafu.
qualitative_descriptor: A powerful and physically imposing stopper, Stam remained a formidable defensive presence in 2004/05 despite being in the veteran stage of his career. He was aggressive in contact and used his immense strength to dominate individual duels, serving as a high-level stopper next to Alessandro Nesta. While he possessed less explosive pace than in his Manchester United prime, his positioning and physical intimidation made him an elite component of Milan's defensive unit.

name: Cristian Brocchi
role_2004_05: A high-energy squad player who provided industrious midfield depth during Milan's run to the final.
qualitative_descriptor: Brocchi was a busy utility midfielder known for his pressing and support running. He served as a reliable role player, offering safe possession and work-rate from the bench.
